title: The Frozen Scriptorium: Insulating a Monastery at the Thermal Min-Cut
strategies: trivial: insulate nothing; the do-nothing envelope (checker baseline, ~0.1)
greedy energy audit: pour layers, one at a time, onto whichever EXTERIOR wall is currently bleeding the most heat; never flag interior doorways (blind to the short-circuit)

strong min-cut allocation: steepest descent on TOTAL network loss over ALL insulatable walls, recomputing each layer's marginal so the budget equalizes marginal resistance along the heater-to-outside min-cut -- often sealing the interior doorway that feeds a window-shorted buffer room

relaxation view: treat effective conductance from the fixed-temperature source set to ground; add resistance where it most raises the cut resistance, accounting for parallel short-circuits (stained-glass windows) that no exterior audit can fix
